Business Formation

Register Your US Business
From Anywhere in the World

Launch your US business the right way — correct entity type, correct state, complete documentation. We guide Indian entrepreneurs and global founders through every step, fully remotely.

LLC Formation
C-Corp / S-Corp

100% Remote
All 50 States

How We Register Your Business

01

Choose Entity Type

We analyze your business goals, tax situation, and investment plans to recommend the ideal structure — LLC, C-Corp, S-Corp, or Partnership.

02

State Filing

We prepare and file your Articles of Organization or Incorporation with the correct state — Delaware, Wyoming, Georgia, or your target state.

03

EIN & Tax Setup

We obtain your Federal EIN (Employer Identification Number) from the IRS, set up your business bank account, and configure your tax elections.

04

Ongoing Compliance

Annual reports, registered agent service, and corporate minutes — we keep your business in good standing year after year.

Choose the Right Structure

Every business is different. We match your goals to the optimal entity type.

LLC (Limited Liability Co.)

  • Most popular for small business
  • Pass-through taxation
  • Flexible management
  • Low compliance burden
  • Foreign ownership allowed

C-Corporation

  • Ideal for VC/investor funding
  • Issue multiple share classes
  • Separate corporate tax
  • Delaware preferred
  • Required for US stock options

S-Corporation

  • Pass-through taxation
  • Reduces self-employment tax
  • US citizens/residents only
  • Max 100 shareholders
  • One share class

Foreign Company Branch

  • Expand your Indian company to US
  • No separate legal entity
  • Parent company liability
  • Simpler than new entity
  • Suitable for testing US market
FAQ

Business Registration FAQs

Everything you need to know about registering a US company.

Yes — non-US citizens and non-residents can form an LLC or C-Corporation in the United States. There are no citizenship or residency requirements. You will need a registered agent in the state of formation and an EIN from the IRS. We handle the entire process remotely.
Delaware is the most popular for C-Corps (especially for startups seeking VC funding) due to its business-friendly courts and laws. Wyoming and Nevada are popular for LLCs due to privacy and low fees. If you'll operate physically in Georgia, registering there avoids foreign qualification fees. We help you choose based on your specific goals.
Most states process standard filings in 5–10 business days. Delaware offers 24-hour expedited processing. Wyoming typically processes in 1–2 business days. Once the state approves your formation, the IRS EIN can be obtained the same day online (for US-based principals) or within 4 weeks by mail (for foreign principals).
An Employer Identification Number (EIN) is your company's federal tax ID, similar to a social security number for businesses. You need it to open a US business bank account, hire employees, file federal taxes, apply for business licenses, and set up payment processing. We obtain your EIN directly from the IRS.
No. You can register a US company entirely remotely. We handle all filings on your behalf. You will need to provide notarized identification documents and sign the formation documents. A US business address (provided by our registered agent service) is required — your personal Indian address is not sufficient.

Ready to Launch Your US Business?

Free 30-minute consultation. We'll recommend the right entity and state for your goals.
